name: Catskill Pickleball Center
description: Catskill Pickleball Center is a place to play pickleball in Catskill, NY. There are 4 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include food, restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, and wheelchair accessibility. A one-time fee is required to play. Reservations are available.
open_play_schedule: Reserve your court online www.catskillgolfclub.com  Or call 518 943 0302
fees: Fee required — Courts $40/hr or 3hrs for $110 (Save $10).  Lessons up to 8 in your group, $40/hr.  Limited number of Pickleball memberships, $300 before June 1.

name: Greene County YMCA
description: Greene County YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Coxsackie, NY. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms, water, and locker rooms.

name: High Ridge Club
description: High Ridge Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Hensonville, NY. There are 3 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like food, locker rooms, restrooms, trainers or lessons, and youth programming. A membership is required to play. The courts cannot be reserved.
open_play_schedule: Pickball everyday lessons and clinics available
fees: Members only — Day passes are available
hours: Weekdays & Weekends 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM The courts are open weather permitting. Tennis/pickleball courts are open dawn to dusk and can be used by

name: Rip Van Winkle Park
description: Rip Van Winkle Park is a place to play pickleball in Tannersville, NY. There are 2 outdoor courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free.
